h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: Learner continues probing the arithmetic behind the CO2 forcing number, moving from percentage skepticism to demanding the origin of the 5.35 coefficient.

scored.recoheredEarlier: If the words are exact, it seems fair, or at least that was the standard in my head. But the headline and pull quote can crop the answer into sounding like a different claim.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: If the words are exact, it seems fair, but that was only testing fabrication, not the frame around it. The earlier mistake was letting clean attribution excuse a crop that makes the source sound more absolute
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was heading toward resolving a practical doubt about whether moving a qualifier is enough, without re-examining the foundational standard of fairness.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the wall map was the thing to trust because Greenland looks almost absurdly large up there.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue correcting the fact without explicitly naming and replacing the earlier framing error.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I think the ward sentence only licenses, sorry, the pattern across admissions in Ward C, not a claim about this patient.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I was still treating the average as if it told us what this patient probably got.
scored.naiveTrajectory: After L2 revoices L1 and reframes the distinction as 'ward figure gives audit question, case note gives case claim,' the expected next turn is to refine the wording choice ('consistent with' vs. 'raises the question') as T3 prompts, not to step back and diagnose a continuing error in framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: "Route is bridge" was my shortcut, but I was still using it like a poster slogan and trying to crown the first twitch as resonance.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: New version: the clamp has to show whether the load actually passes from nose to deck to diagonal to gusset to support, or leaks out as slip, rotation, or a bowed compression bar.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The trajectory pointed toward patiently watching the clamp joint and deferring resonance, continuing the dutiful compliance of L1 and L2.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the person's body gets used to the antibiotic, so I wrote it wrong on the poster.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: That helps, but I still need to keep straight: bacteria change; the patient's body does not become antibiotic-resistant.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue the pattern of seeking clarification on the mechanics of resistance, as in the prior turns' confusion between pre-existing resistance and mutation during treatment.
